XMVDecoder::GetNextFrame

Retrieves the next frame to display from an Xbox Media Video (XMV) file.

HRESULT GetNextFrame(
    IDirect3DSurface8 *pSurface,
    XMVRESULT *pResult,
    DWORD *pTimeOfFrame
);

Parameters

pSurface
[out] Address of an IDirect3DSurface8 interface used to render the frame. The surface must have the same height and width as the video. If no video is desired, pSurface can be NULL.
pResult
[out] Pointer to a member of the XMVRESULT enumerated type, which will receive the status of the new frame.
pTimeOfFrame
[out] Address of a DWORD value that will receive the time (in milliseconds) from the start of the movie to when the returned frame should be displayed. If there is no frame, the value will not change. If the title is allowing the library to synchronize between the video and the audio, pTimeOfFrame may be NULL.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

Either this function or XMVDecoder::Play should be used to play back XMV files. Do not try intermixing the two methods.

If audio is played while this function is in use, DirectSoundDoWork must be called occasionally.

Calling XMVDecoder::TerminateImmediately is guaranteed to call XMVDecoder::GetNextFrame and return an XMVRESULT of XMV_ENDOFFILE.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xmv.h.

Import Library: Use Xmv.lib.

See Also

Playing XMV Files | XMVDecoder::Play, XMVRESULT
